summary: PR 2355 substantially decompiled and cleaned up Kirby SpecialN-related fighter code, with smaller typed-signature cleanup for it_802F23AC in itkirby_2F23. The bot report records +0.09% matched code, +3608 bytes, 16 new matches, and one unmatched-item improvement in ftKb_SpecialN. Evidence is mostly diff plus automated report; there is no PR body, human review, or inline review comment evidence.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; added many Kirby SpecialN/SpecialAirN/SpecialLw animation and IASA bodies, helper inlines, effect spawns, throw/capture callbacks, and matching-oriented stack/local adjustments.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report new matches

key_files.role: Supporting item helper cleanup; changed it_802F23AC from raw int*/float* pointer math to typed Item_GObj*/Vec3* with GET_ITEM and SQ distance calculation.
Header prototype updated to match recovered typed it_802F23AC signature.

what_changed: Added includes for effect async spawning, collision helper access, common items, Dr. Mario pill, and Mario fireball dependencies in ftKb_SpecialN.c.

Moved or introduced static inline helpers for callback setup, Stone IASA logic, Hammer item cleanup/effect spawning, SpecialN hitlag/throw callback setup, capture state entry, spit/drink shared logic, and Dr. Mario pill candidate selection.

Implemented previously placeholder-marked functions including ftKb_SpecialAirLw_IASA, fn_800F53AC, ftKb_SpecialN_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirN_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNCapture0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNCapture_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirNSpit_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialNDrink_Anim.
Improved partially matched functions such as ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5EA8, ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim, fn_800F21E8, and drink item animation variants.
Removed a dont_inline pragma pair around ftKb_SpecialN_800F5DE8 while adding PAD_STACK in several functions to shape stack/codegen.
Updated it_802F23AC to use typed Item_GObj and Vec3 parameters and changed its header declaration accordingly.
smart_moves: The item helper it_802F23AC was converted from raw offset loads through int*/float* casts into typed GET_ITEM(gobj) and Vec3 field access, matching the loaded typed-fields standard.
Shared ground/air SpecialN animation setup was factored into small static inline helpers for motion-state and throw callback setup, reducing repeated callback/flag sequences in the diff.
Squared-distance comparisons were expressed directly, including use of SQ in capture functions, avoiding temporary pointer-cast distance code.
The PR updated the owning item header when the helper signature was recovered, aligning call sites and declaration.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a helper takes an object and position, recovering the real Item_GObj and Vec3 types can remove unsafe pointer casts while preserving a compact distance calculation.
Small static inline helpers can make repeated fighter state setup reviewable while still letting ground and air variants differ only in motion IDs, callback targets, or final transition.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialNSpit_Anim_inline used by ground and air spit animations

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching-oriented constructs such as PAD_STACK, comma-operator fake matches, self-assignments, and empty fake-match branches should be treated as narrow tactics, not style evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK additions in SpecialAirLw_IASA, SpecialN_Anim, SpecialAirN_Anim, spit/drink functions
raw_diff: comments 'fake match, via permuter' and 'FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc'

naming_conventions.name: ftKb_SpecialN_*
naming_conventions.observation: Kirby SpecialN functions keep the existing ftKb_SpecialN and ftKb_SpecialAirN prefix pattern for ground/air variants.

naming_conventions.name: static inline helper suffixes
naming_conventions.observation: New local helpers use descriptive suffixes such as _SetHitlagCb, _SetThrowCb, _EnterState, _SpawnEffect, _CleanupItem, and _Anim_inline.

naming_conventions.name: fp, ip, gobj, pos
naming_conventions.observation: Recovered locals use common short role names for Fighter pointer, Item pointer, object, and position vector when roles are clear.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(20), and padding arrays in ftKb_SpecialN.c

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover helper signatures before preserving raw pointer math
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2355 rewrote it_802F23AC from int*/float* raw offset access to f64 it_802F23AC(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* pos), using GET_ITEM and Vec3 fields, and updated the owning header prototype. This is a concrete example of typed helper recovery improving call sites in a matching PR.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Ground/air fighter variants can share tiny state-transition helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR introduced local static inline helpers for SpecialN hitlag callback setup, throw callback setup, capture state entry, spit handling, and drink handling, then used them from paired ground/air animation functions with only the motion ID, callback target, or final transition differing.

source_update_candidates.text: PR 2355 shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialN.c as the main home for Kirby SpecialN/SpecialAirN animation, capture, spit, drink, Stone IASA, Hammer item/effect handling, and Dr. Mario pill copy-special helper code.

source_update_candidates.title: Fake-match comments and PAD_STACK require evidence tracking
source_update_candidates.text: This PR visibly uses PAD_STACK, fake-match comments, and permuter-driven constructs. Existing standards already cover matching tactics needing evidence; curator may use this PR as an example if examples are added to that standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Not a new standard by itself, but a concrete historical example for an existing standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all PAD_STACK or fake-match constructs in this PR are accepted style; the loaded standards say such tactics need explicit verifier evidence.
Do not infer human reviewer approval or rationale; no human review comments are present.
Do not promote the bot report into proof of every individual tactic's necessity; it only reports aggregate matching progress and listed symbol matches.
